active_projects.description: Fix 'Model not found: opus/' error in .opencode/ agent system after porting from .claude/

artifacts.summary: Research on model not found opus/ error in opencode agent system
Implementation plan with 3 phases to remove invalid model: frontmatter from 34 .opencode/ files
Removed model: frontmatter from 34 .opencode/ files and updated documentation to clarify model: is not supported in OpenCode

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode CLI (v1.14.33) uses parseModel() which splits model strings by "/" into providerID and modelID. Model references must be in "provider/model" format (e.g., "opencode/claude-opus-4-7"). Short aliases like "opus" or "sonnet" (valid in Claude Code) cause "Model not found: opus/" errors because they parse as providerID="opus", modelID="". Command frontmatter "model:" has highest priority in model resolution, overriding session model.

OpenCode model resolution order for slash commands: (1) command frontmatter model field, (2) agent configured model, (3) client session model from TUI, (4) session default. This means any invalid model in frontmatter will always override a valid session model and cause errors immediately.

OpenCode's parseModel() splits model strings by '/' separator. Bare aliases like 'opus' produce {providerID: 'opus', modelID: ''} causing 'Model not found: opus/' errors. When porting .claude/ to .opencode/, always remove the model: frontmatter line entirely. OpenCode uses the session model from the TUI picker instead of per-file model declarations.

active_projects.completion_summary: Removed invalid model: frontmatter from all 34 .opencode/ command and agent files to fix Model not found opus/ error, and updated documentation to document that model: is a Claude Code-only field not supported in OpenCode.

Added lean-lsp MCP server to global OpenCode config (via Home Manager dotfiles), enabled MCP tools for lean agents in per-project config, and updated all agent prompts and permissions from Claude Code naming convention (mcp__lean-lsp__) to OpenCode convention (lean-lsp_). Fixed incorrect package manager (npx -> uvx). Verified server connects successfully.

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode and Claude Code use completely different MCP server configuration formats. Claude Code uses mcpServers key in settings.json with {command, args} objects. OpenCode uses mcp key in opencode.json with {type: local, command: [array]} objects. Tool naming also differs: Claude Code uses mcp__servername__toolname double-underscore convention, OpenCode uses servername_toolname single-underscore.

OpenCode MCP tool naming convention: tools are named as 'servername_toolname' (single underscore separator), defined in Go source as fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s", b.mcpName, b.tool.Name). This differs from Claude Code's 'mcp__servername__toolname' (double underscore with mcp prefix). When configuring OpenCode agents, use 'servername_*' glob patterns for tool enablement, and reference tools as 'servername_toolname' in agent prompts.

The global OpenCode config at ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json is managed by Home Manager via a Nix module at ~/.dotfiles/modules/opencode.nix, sourcing from ~/.dotfiles/config/opencode.json. Edits must be made to the dotfiles source, not the symlinked target, followed by 'home-manager switch --flake . -b backup' to apply changes.

The lean-lsp-mcp package is a Python package installed via uvx (not npm/npx). The correct MCP server command for OpenCode config is ["uvx", "lean-lsp-mcp"], not ["npx", "-y", "lean-lsp-mcp@latest"]. The Claude Code .mcp.json also uses uvx for this server.

active_projects.description: Investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills to enable CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing, following corrected understanding of fork mechanics from official Claude Code docs

artifacts.summary: Revised plan to investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills, enabling FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing with 4 phases: audit, pilot, rollout, documentation

Definitive architecture analysis: hybrid inline-agent approach has no precedent, context: fork + orchestration IS proven (slide skills), cache sharing is fundamentally incompatible with named agent routing, recommend rescoping task 500 to documentation-only

memory_candidates.content: All loaded extension skills (neovim-research, neovim-implementation, nix-research, nix-implementation) use the same Pattern A delegation as core skills -- explicit Task tool with subagent_type and multi-stage postflight. They do NOT use context: fork or agent: frontmatter. Only unloaded extension skills (e.g., slide-planning, slide-critic) use Pattern B with context: fork.

The present extension's skill-slide-planning and skill-slide-critic skills use context: fork + agent: in frontmatter AND explicit subagent_type in their Task call. This proves that context: fork with multi-stage orchestration (preflight, interactive Q&A, delegation, postflight) works correctly.
Claude Code agent definitions support a skills field that preloads skill content into the subagent context at startup. This is described as the inverse of context: fork.

active_projects.description: Optimize team-mode skills (team-research, team-plan, team-implement) to maximize FORK_SUBAGENT parallel cache sharing benefits for teammates 2-N

artifacts.summary: Team-mode skills are already eligible for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing; optimization focuses on prompt restructuring, dynamic token multiplier calculation, and cache-aware metadata reporting.
4-phase plan to restructure team-mode prompts for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing, replace hardcoded token multiplier with dynamic formula, and update documentation

memory_candidates.content: TeammateTool spawns in team-mode skills do NOT specify subagent_type, making them eligible for CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing. Teammates 2-N inherit the parent's prompt cache prefix, reducing input token cost by ~90%. Both CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 must be enabled. The actual cache benefit depends on the byte-identical shared prefix ratio -- prompts should put shared content first and teammate-specific assignments last to maximize overlap.

skill-team-research hardcodes team_size=4 in Stage 1 regardless of user-provided --team-size flag. The four fixed roles are: Primary (A), Alternatives (B), Critic (C), Horizons (D). The token_usage_multiplier is hardcoded at 5.0 in all team skill metadata but should be dynamically calculated.

active_projects.description: Create a unified two-stage Telescope picker triggered by <C-CR> that replaces the current separate <C-CR> (Claude Code) and <C-g> (OpenCode) keybindings. Stage 1 presents a tool picker (Claude Code vs OpenCode) that remembers the last selection and shows it first. Stage 2 presents a session management picker (new session, restore last, browse all) adapted to the selected tool.
